title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Professor Adler review a stack of investigative reports regarding the deaths of Andrew Van and Thomas Braid. The autopsy of Andrew Van revealed no significant findings, and an investigation by the district attorney concluded there was no relationship between Van and the murdered millionaire Thomas Braid, despite similarities in their past correspondence. Dr. Rumsen’s report on Braid indicated no signs of violence, poisoning, or asphyxiation, suggesting Braid was killed by a gunshot or a blunt object to the head.

The investigation into the killer's escape route proved fruitless; police could not identify suspicious travelers by train, car, or boat in the Gulf of Ketchum on the Tuesday of the crime. Regarding the totem pole where Braid was found, the ropes used to bind him were identified as ordinary clotheslines, and experts noted the knots were clumsy but effective. Additionally, the background of a man who joined an Egyptology group in Pittsburgh is unknown.

Ellery discusses the suspicious Rein couple, Percy and Elizabeth, noting that Scotland Yard has been unable to find matching records despite their claims of British citizenship. Further findings reveal that a woman resembling Mrs. Brad left the Park Theatre alone on Tuesday night. A deposit check for renting Oyster Island was written by Vilga Croxall in Peoria, Illinois. While the Brad-Megal Company appears financially stable, Ellery notes that Stephen Megalos has been idle at sea for five years. Finally, Ellery discovers a suppressed report regarding Vilga Krossak, revealing that Krossak was an immigrant from Montenegro who arrived in America around the time of the Tutankhamun's tomb incident.
